@graph.headline: The Economics of Retention: Why the Sale is Only the Midpoint
@graph.description: How sacrificing per-visit margin for volume and long-term advocacy turned a small Alabama dental clinic into a multi-state giant.

acceptedAnswer.text: NRR is a metric that measures how much your revenue grows (or shrinks) from your existing customer base over time. It accounts for retention, upsells, and churn. In a Unified business, NRR is often a more important growth driver than new customer acquisition.

mainEntity.name: How do I calculate Customer Lifetime Value (CLV)?

acceptedAnswer.text: CLV is the present value of the future cash flows attributed to the entire relationship with a customer. Small businesses should start by assigning every transaction to a specific customer and tracking their buying frequency and total spend over time.

mainEntity.name: Why is 'Advocacy' considered part of the revenue journey?

acceptedAnswer.text: Advocacy is the stage where a customer becomes a volunteer for your brand. This could be through word-of-mouth, referrals, or, as in the Alabama dental case, active support for the business's broader mission. Advocacy is the ultimate 'low-CAC' growth lever.
